intro: Geissorhiza tenella, also known as the delicate corkscrew iris, is a small, bulbous perennial plant native to South Africa, characterized by its delicate, twisted leaves and vibrant purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Geissorhiza tenella include iridoids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Geissorhiza tenella is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in combination with other plants to treat various ailments.
